text: Do you know if the flight is delayed?
I was wondering whether you have been here before.
What made you decide to come here?
You look like you could use a coffee. Long layover?
Four hours, and the jet lag is starting to hit. I'm Marcelo, by the way. Are you heading home, or just passing through?
Just passing through, on my way to Oslo. I was wondering whether you know if there is anywhere decent to eat around here.
Funny you should ask. Someone told me about a little place upstairs, a real hidden gem. Word of mouth is the only way you would ever find it.
Perfect. So what takes you to Iceland, business or pleasure?
Pleasure, mostly. I am a first-timer here, and I have always wanted to see the landscapes. What about you, what made you decide to travel through Oslo?
Are you heading home, or just passing through?
Do you know if there is anywhere decent to eat around here?
The best places I find are always word of mouth.

Hi Marcelo, it's Nadia, from the layover in Reykjavik. I finally found that little place you told me about upstairs. You were right, it was a real hidden gem. I never would have found it on my own. Funny how one small conversation at a boarding gate can change a whole trip. I kept thinking about what you said, that word of mouth is worth more than any guidebook. Anyway, I made my flight to Oslo, the jet lag is gone, and I wanted to say thank you for breaking the ice. Most people just stare at their phones and wait. Safe travels, and enjoy Iceland. You are going to love it.

Welcome back to the show. Today, the art of small talk. Here is something most people get wrong: they think small talk is about the weather, but it is really about reading the room. The best conversationalists are not the ones who talk the most. They are the ones who ask a good question and then actually listen to the answer. Notice how a simple follow-up, like what made you decide to do that, opens a door that a yes or no question keeps shut. And do not fear the awkward silence. A short pause is not a failure. It gives the other person room to think. So next time you are stuck at an airport, do not reach for your phone. Break the ice. You never know where the conversation will go.
A good question is the easiest way to break the ice.
Do you know if the flight is delayed?
I was wondering whether you have been here before.
Can you tell me where the gate is?
What made you decide to travel alone?
We found common ground right away.
Are you heading home, or just passing through?
Do you know if there is anywhere decent to eat around here?
What made you decide to come here?
The best places I find are always word of mouth.
